title: plantago lanata var. erectifolia
intro: Plantago lanata var. erectifolia, commonly known as Indian plantain, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the plantain family and is native to the mountainous regions of India and other parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of Indian plantain include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine for treating various health issues. The health benefits of consuming Indian plantain include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and even help lower blood pressure. Indian plantain is often prepared in herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be consumed raw or dried as a powder for its medicinal benefits.
